key_insight: The paper proves optimal H1-norm error estimates for first- and second-order A-stable backward difference full discretizations of Willmore flow on closed surfaces.
engineering_relevance: It provides theoretical reliability guarantees for surface finite element simulations of curvature-driven interface evolution, which is important for high-fidelity geometric PDE solvers.
method: Evolve a coupled second-order system for Willmore flow using surface finite elements of polynomial degree at least two.
Discretize time with backward difference methods of order 1 or 2 that are A-stable.
Prove stability and convergence via energy estimates, Dahlquist G-stability, and Nevanlinna-Odeh multiplier techniques.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a convergence analysis with optimal-order H1 error estimates and numerical experiments that illustrate and complement the theory.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on higher-order spatial elements and careful stability assumptions, so implementation must preserve the coupled structure and regularity requirements.
paper_title: Error estimates for $A$-stable backward difference full discretizations of Willmore flow of closed surfaces
